questions.question: Define the basic components of a block cipher.
keyPoints: Block size: The number of bits in each block, typically denoted as n.
Key size: The number of bits in the secret key, typically denoted as k.

questions.question: Compare and contrast the Substitution-Permutation Network (SPN) and Feistel Network structures.
keyPoints: SPN structure: Consists of multiple rounds of substitution and permutation layers.
Feistel Network structure: Consists of multiple rounds of substitution and permutation layers, with an additional XOR operation.
Security: Feistel Network is more secure than SPN.
Efficiency: Feistel Network is more efficient than SPN.

questions.question: Explain the steps involved in the SPN structure.
Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of the Feistel Network structure compared to the SPN structure.
keyPoints: Advantages of Feistel Network: More secure and efficient than SPN.
Disadvantages of Feistel Network: More complex than SPN.
Trade-off between security and efficiency in block cipher design.

questions.question: Explain with example how the DES (Data Encryption Standard) algorithm works.
keyPoints: DES is a classic example of a Feistel Network block cipher.
Uses a 64-bit block size and a 56-bit key size.
Consists of 16 rounds of substitution and permutation layers, with an additional XOR operation.
